sections.paragraph:
γλυπτός, ή, όν carved (γλύφω ‘carve’; Theophr., Lap. 5 et al.; LXX) τὸ γ. carved image (Judg 18:18 al. and freq. pl. in LXX; ViElisha 3 [p. 95, 5 Sch.]; loanw. in rabb.) B 12:6 (Dt 27:15).—TW.

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

  1. pl. = plural

Sch. Sch. = TSchermann, Prophetarum vitae fabulosae—List 5

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 201
